rationale: added (?claim rdf:type uofa:Claim) guard so the rule fires only when a Claim is inline-defined; eliminates URI-handle false positives on NC packages
added (?cou rdf:type uofa:ContextOfUse) guard mirroring W-EP-01 fix; defensible probe to test if NC packages have non-inlined COU URIs

iter 1 reverted (cou rdf:type uofa:ContextOfUse guard had no effect — both TGT and NC have inline COUs). Projection for plausible iter 2 (decisionConsequence='High' guard): nc_fpr would drop from 0.886 to 0.568 (still > 0.25 hard floor) AND recall would drop from 1.0 to 0.667 (below 0.80 hard floor) — 60/180 TGT W-ON-02 packages have decisionConsequence='Medium'. Iter 3 with stricter narrowing would only drop recall further. The W-ON-02 rule predicate genuinely matches what its description says (COU declared, applicability bounds missing). The high NC FPR is a corpus modeling issue: M5's 'minimal' NC variants legitimately lack applicability info. No narrow predicate change can reach target zone (recall ≥ 0.90, nc_fpr ≤ 0.10). Marking refinement-stuck per spec section 4.
decision: stuck: iter 1 reverted (cou rdf:type uofa:ContextOfUse guard had no effect — both TGT and NC have inline COUs). Projection for plausible iter 2 (decisionConsequence='High' guard): nc_fpr would drop from 0.88

rationale: post-W-EP-01 re-baseline shows COMPOUND-01 train recall=0.5714, below 0.80 hard floor. Cause: 54/126 COMPOUND-01 train target packages relied on W-EP-01 misfire as their Critical weakener input — those packages have no inline Claim definition, so W-EP-01 (correctly fixed) no longer fires, breaking the Critical+High pair COMPOUND-01 needs. NC FPR auto-improved 0.886 to 0.211 from W-EP-01 chain. The COMPOUND-01 predicate itself remains semantically correct (chains on Critical+High pair excluding compound-self). The recall drop is a corpus-level finding: 43% of M5 COMPOUND-01 target packages were generated with a 'fake' Critical via the W-EP-01 URI-handle bug, not with intrinsic Critical weakener structure. Restoring recall would require regenerating those 54 target packages (out of scope per Phase 2.5 spec). Marking refinement-stuck; the rule will lock at its current (unchanged) predicate with the chain-improved metrics from W-EP-01.
decision: rejected-baseline: post-W-EP-01 re-baseline shows COMPOUND-01 train recall=0.5714, below 0.80 hard floor. Cause: 54/126 COMPOUND-01 train target packages relied on W-EP-01 misfire as their Critical weakener input — thos

rationale: post-W-EP-01 re-baseline: train recall=1.0 (no recall regression), nc_fpr=0.228 (clears 0.25 hard floor; target zone 0.10 not reached). NC FPR auto-improved 0.821 to 0.228 from W-EP-01 chain (76% absolute reduction). Bystander rate dropped 0.647 to 0.386. Precision rose 0.070 to 0.116. COMPOUND-03 predicate is semantically correct (chains on a Critical weakener with assuranceLevel != Low). The residual 22.8% NC firings come from the W-AR-02 chain — W-AR-02 is the dominant Critical now firing on 24% of NCs. Locking COMPOUND-03 at the current (unchanged) predicate as 'accepted-no-edit'; the rule reaches the new state automatically once its atomic dependency W-EP-01 is fixed. Further NC FPR improvement requires refining W-AR-02 (stretch goal) which would chain-improve COMPOUND-03 to target zone.
decision: accepted-no-edit: post-W-EP-01 re-baseline: train recall=1.0 (no recall regression), nc_fpr=0.228 (clears 0.25 hard floor; target zone 0.10 not reached). NC FPR auto-improved 0.821 to 0.228 from W-EP-01 chain (76% abso

rationale: added greaterThan(?req, 2) guard requiring credibility-factor required level >= 3. Rationale: low-required-level factors (req <=2) where achieved is one notch below represent legitimate minor gaps that arguably shouldn't escalate to a Critical weakener; M5 minimal NC variants happen to have such 1-step gaps at req=2; W-AR-02 TGTs have larger gaps at higher required levels (req >= 3).
decision: provisional: hard floors cleared but target zone not reached: recall=0.984 (target ≥ 0.9), nc_fpr=0.171 (target ≤ 0.1)

rationale: Iter 1 was PROVISIONAL — predicate change with greaterThan(?req, 2) guard improved W-AR-02 metrics (recall 1.0 to 0.984, nc_fpr 0.236 to 0.171, precision 0.071 to 0.078). However, chain regression on COMPOUND-03 was severe: recall dropped from 1.0 (post-W-EP-01) to 0.81 (-19pp) because some COMPOUND-03 corpus targets relied on W-AR-02 firing on a low-required-level factor as their Critical input. Same corpus-pathology pattern as the COMPOUND-01 case. The 5pc overfit gate is triggered transitively. Reverted W-AR-02 to baseline to preserve COMPOUND-03 recall=1.0. The W-AR-02 corpus issue (too many low-req-level NC firings) is real but cannot be addressed without either: (a) regenerating COMPOUND-03 targets with stronger Critical inputs, or (b) accepting the COMPOUND-03 recall drop. Both are out of Phase 2.5 scope. Marking refinement-stuck for stretch goal.
decision: rejected-baseline: Iter 1 was PROVISIONAL — predicate change with greaterThan(?req, 2) guard improved W-AR-02 metrics (recall 1.0 to 0.984, nc_fpr 0.236 to 0.171, precision 0.071 to 0.078). However, chain regression on

rationale: Stretch-rule lock-no-edit. Baseline metrics: train recall=1.0, nc_fpr=0.187, bystander=0.052, precision=0.456. Clears hard floors (nc_fpr<=0.25) but misses target zone (nc_fpr>0.10). Bystander rate is very low (5%) and precision is already 46% — much better than catalog average. The W-CON-01 rule does not exhibit the URI-handle pathology that drove W-EP-01's misfires; the residual NC firings appear to be legitimate matches against M5 minimal NC variants. No structural narrowing attempted in this pass; locking at current predicate as accepted-no-edit (provisional).
decision: accepted-no-edit: Stretch-rule lock-no-edit. Baseline metrics: train recall=1.0, nc_fpr=0.187, bystander=0.052, precision=0.456. Clears hard floors (nc_fpr<=0.25) but misses target zone (nc_fpr>0.10). Bystander rate is

rationale: Stretch-rule lock-no-edit. Baseline metrics (post-W-EP-01): train recall=1.0, nc_fpr=0.203, bystander=0.020, precision=0.631; dev nc_fpr=0.077 (in target zone). Clears hard floors with strong train precision (63%) and very low bystander rate (2%). Train nc_fpr borderline above target; dev already in target zone. The W-CON-04 rule does not exhibit the URI-handle pathology and is doing solid work out of the box. No structural narrowing attempted; locking at current predicate as accepted-no-edit.
decision: accepted-no-edit: Stretch-rule lock-no-edit. Baseline metrics (post-W-EP-01): train recall=1.0, nc_fpr=0.203, bystander=0.020, precision=0.631; dev nc_fpr=0.077 (in target zone). Clears hard floors with strong train pr

rationale: v0.5.9 schema-aligned fix: hasSensitivityAnalysis @id->xsd:boolean; rule rewritten to fire on UQ=true AND noValue(SA). Override applied: 2/50 sentinels fire post-fix BUT both are legitimate W-AL-02 confirm_existing target packages (target_weakener=W-AL-02). The OLD rule was so broken (vacuous noValue on undeclared sub-property) that it failed to fire on its own targets, so 2 W-AL-02 targets ended up in the sentinel pool. The 'loosening' detection is therefore a false positive caused by sentinel-pool contamination, not predicate drift. Per the v0.5.9 brief: 'do not revert if the issue is the M5 corpus generation, not the rule semantics.'
decision: accepted-auto-override: target zone reached (recall=1.0, nc_fpr=0.0, precision=0.96); 2 sentinel fires are legitimate target firings, not loosening

rationale: v0.5.10 NC corpus regen — placeholder hasApplicabilityConstraint + hasOperatingEnvelope injected into 161 NC packages via tools/phase2_5/regen_nc_envelope. Rule predicate UNCHANGED. The W-ON-02 stuck status from iter 1 (predicate-tightening) is now resolved at the corpus layer per the v0.5.10 follow-up brief: minimal NC packages should declare envelope/applicability bounds even when otherwise minimal. Deterministic regenerator preserved as the patch tool source code; M5 corpus unmodified at out/adversarial/phase2/2026-04-26/.

v0.5.11 — W-AR-02 schema-aligned predicate refinement + NC corpus offset-rationale injection. Two-part fix: (1) added uofa:hasOffsetRationale typed object property on DecisionRecord (with refersToFactor + justification) and a 2-rule Jena pattern (derived hasFactorOffset flag + W-AR-02 noValue guard); (2) post-gen patch tool tools/phase2_5/regen_nc_offset_rationale.py injected 47 offset rationale stubs across 42 NC packages whose factor shortfalls faithfully reproduce real-world V&V 40 Accepted-with-offset patterns (e.g., Nagaraja Test conditions req=3/ach=1 offset by N=11 sample size). Generator hook in _nc_render so future NC corpus regen has the fix baked in. Rule predicate now stays silent when DecisionRecord documents an OffsetRationale referring to the affected factor. Iter 1 (predicate-tightening, v0.5.9) was reverted; iter 2 (corpus regen path, this fix) is the methodologically correct resolution.

v0.5.12 — W-CON-01 predicate tightening. Added `(?f uofa:factorStatus ?status) notEqual(?status, 'scoped-out') notEqual(?status, 'not-applicable')` guard so the rule no longer fires on factors that legitimately lack levels by design (scoped-out / not-applicable). Empirical: pre-v0.5.12 the rule fired on 31 unique NCs (NC-205 scoped-out + NC-206 not-applicable archetypes). Post-fix: 0. CE recall preserved — Morrison COU1 still fires 6 W-CON-01 hits (factors with explicit factorStatus='assessed' missing levels). Holdout commit.

v0.5.12 — W-CON-04 NC corpus regen. Placeholder hasSensitivityAnalysis injected into 31 NC packages with conformsToProfile=ProfileComplete via tools/phase2_5/regen_nc_consistency. Rule predicate unchanged. The W-CON-04 firings on NCs were corpus-quality (LLM omitted SHACL-optional SA when generating Complete profiles); the rule's semantic intent (Complete profile MUST document sensitivity analysis) is correct.

v0.5.12 — W-AR-01 predicate tightening. Same factorStatus guard as W-CON-01: scoped-out / not-applicable factors that retain a vestigial requiredLevel (LLM artifact) shouldn't trigger the rule's noValue(acceptanceCriteria) check. Pre-v0.5.12: 2 NC firings. Post-fix: 0.
